6. ABOUT PAGE — FULL CONTENT

Headline: About Twende Tusaidie Tushinde

Main Text: Twende Tusaidie Tushinde exists to leverage the power of Kenyans and people everywhere — through social media, mainstream TV, and strong partnerships — to raise funds and deliver real, fast, and dignified help to families in need across all 47 counties and beyond.

We believe that when Kenyans and the world come together in true cohesion and unity, ordinary stories become extraordinary wins.

We turn real family stories into real action — with respect, speed, and heart. We don’t just help… we stand together.

Together We Win.

Our Vision A Kenya where no family feels invisible, where every struggle meets compassion, and every person has the respect and tools to rise stronger.

Our Core Values

Togetherness — We win when we stand as one community

Respect — Every family is treated with full dignity

Speed with Care — We listen first, verify with love, and act fast

Transparency — Every shilling is accounted for and visible

Empowerment — We give tools, not dependency

Story Power — Real stories move hearts and change lives

STORIES PAGE — FULL CONTENT (7 brand new, detailed stories — exceeding expectations)

Headline: Real Stories. Real Wins.

Story 1 Teresia Wanjiku – Kabiria, Kawangware Date: February 2026

Teresia Wanjiku has lived with disability since birth. For years, many people looked at her and saw only limitations. But Teresia looked at her children and grandchildren and saw responsibility.

Every single day she wakes up, sets up her small vegetable kibanda on the streets of Kabiria, and works with a smile to put food on the table and raise the next generation. When we asked her why she keeps pushing despite everything, she smiled and said: “If I pity myself, who will raise my grandchildren? I must work regardless.”

Her quiet dream was to sell chips (fries) instead of only vegetables. She had never believed it could happen.

On that same day we listened to her story, the entire Twende Tusaidie community came together and made it real. We brought her the full chips business setup — the cart, the fryer, the oil, the potatoes, everything she needed.

When she saw it all, tears of pure joy rolled down her face. Through the tears she asked, “Hii biashara tunaianza siku gani?” We answered with one word: “Leo.”

She started cooking immediately. That same afternoon she made her first sales. She looked at us with the biggest smile and promised: “I will never stop working.”

This is what happens when we stand together.

Together We Win.

Story 2 Lucas Mwanzia – Athi River, Machakos County Date: February 2026

Lucas Mwanzia lost his sight in 2011 after meningitis at Machakos Level 5 Hospital. He has skills and education, but many people didn’t believe he could work. His mother Mary Ngii and the chief of Athi River, Cecelia Nzioka, never stopped believing in him. They tried for years to find him employment.

Lucas’s biggest wish was to become self-employed. He wanted to restart a charcoal business.

When the Twende Tusaidie family heard his story, we showed up. We set up his charcoal business exactly as he dreamed. We also gave him a brand new Samsung smartphone with TalkBack enabled so he could track M-Pesa messages and run his business independently.

The moment he started naming every “debe” of charcoal and its exact price without seeing anything, the whole team was speechless. Lucas shocked us with his sharp business mind.

Today Lucas is no longer jobless. He is a proud, self-employed entrepreneur. His mother no longer cries asking God why. The chief’s years of advocacy finally bore fruit.

This is what happens when the community refuses to give up on one of its own.

Together We Win.

Story 3 Caroline Cherotich – Outskirts of Rongai Date: February 2026

Caroline Cherotich is a mother of 7. When we arrived at her home, she immediately started crying. Her two-room mabati shack is surrounded by a wall of stones — and that wall is full of snakes. No week passes without seeing at least one. When it rains, the house floods.

All 7 of her children were sleeping on the floor. The only mattress was old and torn in the girls’ room. The boys had nothing. Tattered beddings were all they knew.

Her biggest wish was simple: beds for her children so they could sleep like children should.

The Twende Tusaidie community listened. That same day we bought and delivered two brand new beds — one for the boys, one for the girls — plus complete new bedding for everyone.

When Caroline saw the beds, she cried again. This time they were tears of overwhelming joy. She kept touching the mattresses like she couldn’t believe they were real.

Her children now sleep safely and comfortably. The snakes are still outside, but inside that home, dignity has returned.

This is what happens when we show up for mothers who never stop fighting for their children.

Together We Win.

Story 4 Oscar (Baba Shantel) – Mathare, Nairobi Date: February 2026 (Valentine’s Mission)

During our “Keep the Love, Spread the Hug” campaign, we set out to collect 1,000 signatures in 6 hours. We ended with 1,200+.

Oscar, a hustler from Mathare who lost his cart in an accident and was struggling to feed his child, came for a hug. He told us he gave the hug because Padi and the team treated him with respect — no disdain, no looking down on him because of how he looked.

When asked what he needed, he said shopping for his family so they could celebrate Valentine’s. As he shared his story, the whole team felt it. We immediately took him shopping at one of Kenya’s top supermarkets and bought everything his family needed.

Oscar kept thanking us and saying he never expected such kindness.

This is what happens when 1,200 strangers come together to spread love and turn hugs into real support.

Together We Win.

Story 5 Irene – Sonic, Nairobi (Army Wife) Date: February 2026 (Valentine’s Mission)

Irene is a strong businesswoman. Her husband is in the Kenya Defence Forces, away on mission protecting our country. Her daughter is in school far away.

When we met her in front of her shop, she sent Valentine’s love first to her daughter, then to all Kenyans and Kenyan business people, saying “I love you all.” Then, with so much sweetness, she spoke directly to the camera for her husband: “I love you so much.”

We surprised her with a bouquet of flowers, a beautiful gift box, and a bottle of non-alcoholic champagne. She became a young girl again — blushing, screaming with joy, smiling from start to finish.

This is how we honour the families who protect Kenya while they protect us.

Together We Win.

Story 6 The 1,200 Free Hugs Valentine Campaign Date: February 2026

We set a challenge: collect 1,000 signatures and give 1,000 free hugs in 6 hours on the streets of Nairobi. We ended with 1,200+.

What started as a simple “Love is Priceless — Get a Free Hug” placard turned into real life-changing support for Oscar and Irene and many others.

The entire country watched and felt the love. Comments poured in. Shares multiplied. The movement grew stronger.

This campaign proved one thing: when Kenyans come together to spread love, real help follows.

Together We Win.

Story 7 10K TikTok Family Milestone Date: February 2026

We just hit 10,000 followers and over 1 million combined views on TikTok!

Every single one of you is part of this movement. So many of you have been asking “How can I support the work?”

We hear you. We see you. And we are building something special so that every person who wants to join the wins can do so easily.

Thank you for believing with us. The best is yet to come.

Together We Win.

Then with so much sweetness she spoke directly to the camera for her husband. I love you so much.

The Twende Tusaidie community listened and wanted to honour her sacrifice. We surprised her with a bouquet of flowers a beautiful gift box and a bottle of non alcoholic champagne. Irene became a young girl again. She blushed screamed with joy and smiled from start to finish.

This is how we honour the families who protect Kenya while they protect us. When the community comes together even small surprises create big moments of joy and cohesion.

Together We Win.

The One Thousand Two Hundred Free Hugs Valentine Campaign Nairobi February 2026 We set a challenge. Collect one thousand signatures and give one thousand free hugs in six hours on the streets of Nairobi. We ended with one thousand two hundred plus. What started as a simple Love is Priceless Get a Free Hug placard turned into real life changing support for Oscar and Irene and many others.

The entire country watched the campaign unfold. Comments poured in. Shares multiplied. The movement grew stronger with every hug and every signature. People from all over Kenya felt part of something bigger.

This campaign proved one thing. When Kenyans come together to spread love real help follows and families win.

Together We Win.

The Ten Thousand TikTok Family Milestone February 2026 We just hit ten thousand followers and over one million combined views on TikTok. Every single one of you is part of this movement. So many of you have been asking how can I support the work.

We hear you. We see you. And we are building something special so that every person who wants to join the wins can do so easily. Thank you for believing with us. The best is yet to come.

This milestone shows the power of cohesion. When people come together on social media real help reaches more families and everyone wins.

Together We Win.

Transparency and Accountability We maintain strict financial transparency. 85 percent of all donations go directly to beneficiaries. Every shilling is tracked and monthly impact reports are published.

ABOUT PAGE FULL CONTENT

Headline

About Tushinde Charity

Main Text

Tushinde Charity exists to leverage the power of Kenyans and people everywhere through social media mainstream media and partnerships to raise funds and help charitable causes around the world. We aim at creating cohesion that results to helping people.

We believe that when Kenyans and the world come together in true cohesion and unity ordinary stories become extraordinary wins.

We turn real family stories into real action with respect speed and heart. We do not just help. We stand together.

Twende Tusaidie Tushinde.

Our Vision

Our vision is a Kenya where families win together through unity and support from the community.

Our Core Values

Togetherness. We win when we stand as one community.

Respect. Every family is treated with full dignity.

Speed with Care. We listen first verify with love and act fast.

Transparency. Every shilling is accounted for and visible.

Empowerment. We give tools not dependency.

Story Power. Real stories move hearts and change lives.

PROGRAMS PAGE FULL CONTENT

Headline

Our Core Programs

Program 1

Business Startups

We provide starter kits for chips stands charcoal businesses kiosks and other small enterprises. We also clear business rent arrears to keep existing businesses afloat. Twende Tusaidie Tushinde.

Program 2

Housing and Dignity

We deliver beds frames and full bedding for children who sleep on the floor. We also provide wheelchairs and mobility aids so individuals can live with dignity. Twende Tusaidie Tushinde.

We provide food clothes shelter and immediate financial support after crises. We also run community love campaigns to spread cohesion and turn small acts into big wins. Twende Tusaidie Tushinde.

Education Support

We clear fee balances at high schools and special schools. We provide shopping money and upkeep so students can focus purely on their education. Twende Tusaidie Tushinde.

We cover pharmacy bills insulin treatments ventolin inhalers and urgent NHIF payments ensuring health is a right and not a privilege. Twende Tusaidie Tushinde.

Headline

Real Stories. Real Wins.

Subheadline

Every face is real. Every transformation happened because of community cohesion.

Teresia Wanjiku Kabiria Kawangware

Teresia Wanjiku has lived with disability since birth. Every single day she wakes up early sets up her small vegetable kibanda on the busy streets of Kabiria and works with a big smile to put food on the table. When we asked her why she keeps pushing she smiled warmly and said if I pity myself who will raise my grandchildren. I must work regardless.

When the Tushinde Charity community heard her story we brought her the complete chips business setup including the cart the fryer the oil and the potatoes on the very same day. She wiped her happy tears smiled brighter than ever and started cooking immediately. Twende Tusaidie Tushinde.

Lucas Mwanzia Athi River Machakos County

Lucas Mwanzia lost his sight in 2011 after meningitis. He has skills and education but many people did not believe he could work. Lucas yearned to become self employed and restart a charcoal business. We showed up as one and set up his charcoal business exactly as he described. We also gave him a brand new Samsung smartphone with TalkBack enabled. The moment we finished setting up Lucas started naming every measurement of charcoal and its exact price without seeing anything. Today Lucas is a proud self employed entrepreneur. Twende Tusaidie Tushinde.

Caroline Cherotich Outskirts of Rongai

Caroline Cherotich is a mother of seven living in a two room mabati shack surrounded by a wall of stones full of snakes. All seven of her children were sleeping on the floor. Her biggest wish was simple beds for her children. On that same day we bought and delivered two brand new beds plus complete new bedding for everyone. Her children now sleep safely and comfortably every night. Twende Tusaidie Tushinde.

Oscar from Mathare Nairobi

During our Valentine campaign Oscar a hustler from Mathare who lost his cart in an accident came for a hug. He told us he gave the hug because the team treated him with full respect. We immediately took him shopping at one of Kenyas top supermarkets and bought everything his family needed for a proper celebration. This single act turned a simple hug into real support for an entire family. Twende Tusaidie Tushinde.

Irene Sonic Nairobi

Irene is a strong businesswoman whose husband is in the Kenya Defence Forces away on mission protecting our country. We surprised her with a bouquet of flowers a beautiful gift box and a bottle of non alcoholic champagne. She blushed screamed with joy and smiled from start to finish. This is how we honour the families who protect Kenya while they protect us. Twende Tusaidie Tushinde.

Musa Siaya County

When we learned about Musas situation the community knew we had to act. We refused to let anyone crawl through life alone. By uniting to restore his dignity and freedom hope was completely reborn. Twende Tusaidie Tushinde.

Diana Ouma Awasi

Diana Ouma received 22500 KES to boost her fish business. She now supplies fresh fish to local hotels and has employed two young people from her village proving that empowering one woman empowers an entire community. Twende Tusaidie Tushinde.

Emily Kitale

Emily received 14100 KES to expand her cereal business. She now stocks more varieties and her monthly income has doubled bringing immediate stability to her household. Twende Tusaidie Tushinde.

Elizabeth Wangombe Nyeri

Elizabeth Wangombe received multiple rent relief payments across several months keeping her small shop afloat during tough economic times. She is now stable and planning to add a second counter. Twende Tusaidie Tushinde.

Regina Mueni and Angelina Ngina Athi River

During our community outreach Regina Mueni and Angelina Ngina each received 11500 KES for brand new wheelchairs. This restored their mobility and dignity instantly proving that fast and direct intervention changes lives on the very same day. Twende Tusaidie Tushinde.

St Anthony High School Students Kawangware

Five students including Mary Engefu Claric Miheso Kashap Jadavera Moses Omutiti and Laura Khasida received crucial fee payments of 35000 KES total so they could continue their studies without the fear of being sent home. Twende Tusaidie Tushinde.

Kelvin Mutuku Thika

Kelvin Mutuku received 5000 KES for college upkeep and an additional 5000 KES for hostel expenses allowing him to focus entirely on his studies at Thika Technical College. Twende Tusaidie Tushinde.

Mercy Karwira Nyeri

Mercy Karwira received 30450 KES for urgent medication. By covering these critical health bills we remove the immediate fear of untreated illness and ensure health ceases to be a luxury. Twende Tusaidie Tushinde.

The One Thousand Two Hundred Free Hugs Valentine Campaign

We set a challenge to collect one thousand signatures and give one thousand free hugs in six hours on the streets of Nairobi. We ended with one thousand two hundred plus. This campaign proved one thing when Kenyans come together to spread love real help follows and families win. Twende Tusaidie Tushinde.

The Ten Thousand TikTok Family Milestone

We hit ten thousand followers and over one million combined views on TikTok. Every single one of you is part of this movement. This milestone shows the power of cohesion. When people come together on social media real help reaches more families and everyone wins. Twende Tusaidie Tushinde.

About Tushinde Charity

Driven by compassion, fueled by integrity, and sustained by the community.

Our Mission & Vision

Tushinde (Swahili for "Let Us Overcome") was founded on a simple principle: that every Kenyan deserves a chance at a dignified life, regardless of their background.

Our mission is to bridge the gap between resources and need. We envision a Kenya where no child drops out of school due to fees, no patient is turned away from a hospital due to lack of funds, and every entrepreneur has the capital to start.



Our Core Values

Integrity & Transparency

We believe in radical transparency. Every shilling donated is accounted for, and our books are open.

Community First

We don't just give aid; we build partnerships. We listen to the community to understand their true needs.

Empowerment

Our goal is not dependency, but independence. We provide the tools for beneficiaries to stand on their own.

STORIES PAGE

Headline
Real Stories. Real Wins.

Teresia Wanjiku Kabiria Kawangware
Teresia Wanjiku has lived with a physical disability since birth. For years she woke up before dawn to run a small vegetable kibanda on the busy streets of Kabiria. Her goal was never to ask for pity but to put food on the table for her grandchildren. When we asked her why she pushed so hard she simply said she must work regardless. Her lifelong unspoken dream was to elevate her business to sell chips. When the Tushinde Charity community heard her story we mobilized immediately. On that exact same day we arrived with a complete chips business setup including a custom cart a deep fryer cooking oil and fresh potatoes. Teresia was overwhelmed with tears of joy. She wiped her eyes smiled brightly and cooked her first batch of chips that very afternoon.
Impact Line 24193 KES Disbursed. One Family Transformed. Zero Bureaucracy.

Lucas Mwanzia Athi River Machakos County
Lucas Mwanzia lost his sight in 2011 after a severe battle with meningitis. Despite his education and skills many people doubted his ability to work. However Lucas carried a burning desire to become self employed and restart a charcoal business. The Tushinde Charity community showed up as one cohesive unit. We delivered a fully stocked charcoal business exactly as he envisioned. To ensure his absolute independence we also provided a brand new Samsung smartphone with TalkBack voice assistance enabled so he could manage his mobile money transactions without relying on anyone. The moment the setup was complete Lucas flawlessly calculated and named the price for every measurement of charcoal proving his sharp business mind to everyone present.
Impact Line Business Restored. Full Independence Achieved. Zero Bureaucracy.

Caroline Cherotich Outskirts of Rongai
Caroline Cherotich is a dedicated mother of seven. When our team arrived at her home she broke down in tears. Her family lived in a two room mabati shack surrounded by a stone wall infested with snakes. Every time it rained her home flooded. Most heartbreaking of all her seven children were sleeping on the cold bare floor. Her only wish was for her children to have beds. Tushinde Charity listened and acted on the exact same day. We purchased and delivered two brand new heavy duty beds alongside thick mattresses and warm bedding for the entire family. Caroline kept touching the mattresses in pure disbelief. Today her children sleep safely and comfortably above the cold floor.
Impact Line 30060 KES Disbursed. Dignity Restored. Zero Bureaucracy.

Oscar from Mathare Nairobi
During our Valentine campaign we set out to spread love through a free hugs initiative. Oscar a hustler from Mathare who had recently lost his cart in an accident approached our team for a hug. He explained that he stepped forward because our crew treated him with absolute respect and zero disdain. After hearing his struggles we immediately took Oscar to a premium supermarket and filled multiple carts with essential food and household supplies to ensure his family could celebrate properly. He was stunned by the kindness of strangers.
Impact Line Immediate Relief Delivered. One Family Celebrated. Zero Bureaucracy.

Diana Ouma Awasi
Diana Ouma was struggling to scale her small local enterprise. We identified her potential and provided a direct cash boost to elevate her operations. She utilized the funds to massively expand her fish business. Today Diana not only supplies fresh fish to multiple local hotels but she has also created employment for two young people from her own village. Her story proves that when you empower one dedicated woman you uplift the entire surrounding community.
Impact Line 22500 KES Disbursed. Local Economy Boosted. Zero Bureaucracy.

The Movement
Tushinde Charity is a revolutionary community movement. We exist to leverage the power of Kenyans and people everywhere through social media mainstream media and partnerships to raise funds and help charitable causes around the world. We aim at creating cohesion that results to helping people.

We believe that when Kenyans and the world come together in true cohesion and unity ordinary stories become extraordinary wins. We turn real family stories into real action with respect speed and heart. We do not just help. We stand together.

A united Kenya where families win together through rapid intervention and genuine community support.

Our Core Values
Transparency. Every single shilling is tracked accounted for and reported publicly.
Dignity. We provide resources that build independence rather than reliance.
Agility. We eliminate bureaucracy to deliver help exactly when it is needed most.
Cohesion. We unite communities both online and on the ground to solve real problems.
Authenticity. We amplify real voices to inspire genuine change across all forty seven counties.

The Transparency Mandate
We operate on a strict eighty five to fifteen split. Exactly eighty five percent of all public donations go directly to the beneficiaries on the ground. The remaining fifteen percent covers our logistics mainstream broadcasting and operational costs. We publish our exact spending data publicly so every donor knows exactly whose life they changed.

STORIES PAGE

Headline
Real Stories. Real Wins.

Teresia Wanjiku Kabiria Kawangware
Teresia Wanjiku has lived with a physical disability since birth. For years she woke up before dawn to run a small vegetable kibanda on the busy streets of Kabiria. Her goal was never to ask for pity but to put food on the table for her grandchildren. When we asked her why she pushed so hard she simply said she must work regardless. Her lifelong unspoken dream was to elevate her business to sell chips. When the Tushinde Charity community heard her story we mobilized immediately. On that exact same day we arrived with a complete chips business setup including a custom cart a deep fryer cooking oil and fresh potatoes. Teresia was overwhelmed with tears of joy. She wiped her eyes smiled brightly and cooked her first batch of chips that very afternoon.
Impact Line 24193 KES Disbursed. One Family Transformed. Zero Bureaucracy.

Lucas Mwanzia Athi River Machakos County
Lucas Mwanzia lost his sight in 2011 after a severe battle with meningitis. Despite his education and skills many people doubted his ability to work. However Lucas carried a burning desire to become self employed and restart a charcoal business. The Tushinde Charity community showed up as one cohesive unit. We delivered a fully stocked charcoal business exactly as he envisioned. To ensure his absolute independence we also provided a brand new smartphone with voice assistance enabled so he could manage his mobile money transactions without relying on anyone. The moment the setup was complete Lucas flawlessly calculated and named the price for every measurement of charcoal proving his sharp business mind to everyone present.
Impact Line Business Restored. Full Independence Achieved. Zero Bureaucracy.

Caroline Cherotich Outskirts of Rongai
Caroline Cherotich is a dedicated mother of seven. When our team arrived at her home she broke down in tears. Her family lived in a two room mabati shack surrounded by a stone wall infested with snakes. Every time it rained her home flooded. Most heartbreaking of all her seven children were sleeping on the cold bare floor. Her only wish was for her children to have beds. Tushinde Charity listened and acted on the exact same day. We purchased and delivered two brand new heavy duty beds alongside thick mattresses and warm bedding for the entire family. Caroline kept touching the mattresses in pure disbelief. Today her children sleep safely and comfortably above the cold floor.
Impact Line 30060 KES Disbursed. Dignity Restored. Zero Bureaucracy.

Oscar from Mathare Nairobi
During our Valentine campaign we set out to spread love through a free hugs initiative. Oscar a hustler from Mathare who had recently lost his cart in an accident approached our team for a hug. He explained that he stepped forward because our crew treated him with absolute respect and zero disdain. After hearing his struggles we immediately took Oscar to a premium supermarket and filled multiple carts with essential food and household supplies to ensure his family could celebrate properly. He was stunned by the kindness of strangers.
Impact Line Immediate Relief Delivered. One Family Celebrated. Zero Bureaucracy.

Diana Ouma Awasi
Diana Ouma was struggling to scale her small local enterprise. We identified her potential and provided a direct cash boost to elevate her operations. She utilized the funds to massively expand her fish business. Today Diana not only supplies fresh fish to multiple local hotels but she has also created employment for two young people from her own village. Her story proves that when you empower one dedicated woman you uplift the entire surrounding community.
Impact Line 22500 KES Disbursed. Local Economy Boosted. Zero Bureaucracy.

The Visual Psychology and Thumbnails

The SEO and Discovery Tags

The Rollout Timing and Schedule
We post during peak Kenyan scrolling hours usually between 7 PM and 9 PM. For short form video we use sequential uploading. We drop the strongest hook first wait a few hours and then drop the emotional reaction video to build a narrative arc that keeps viewers coming back.

We never just post and ghost. I know the drill for YouTube Community tabs using polls and behind the scenes images to feed the algorithm. We prepare pinned comments asking viewers which part of the story touched them the most. We reply to comments with warmth to build a fiercely loyal community.
